original_abstract: Since the emergence of primary amine-thiourea organocatalysis in 2006, extensive research has established these bifunctional scaffolds as powerful tools for asymmetric synthesis. These catalysts have proven remarkably versatile, enabling a broad spectrum of highly enantioselective transformations that yield complex, functionalized organic molecules. This review provides a comprehensive overview of the field's evolution, highlighting recent breakthroughs and the expanding utility of primary amine-based thioureas in modern synthetic chemistry.
humanized_abstract: When the primary amine-thiourea organocatalysis concept was introduced in 2006, a growing body of literature has demonstrated that bifunctional catalysts based on this theme are highly efficient for numerous asymmetric transformations leading to complex chiral organic molecules. Today, the use of primary amine-based thioureas as organocatalysts in asymmetric catalysis has demonstrated their versatile application in achieving a plethora of highly enantioselective reactions. This article aims to give an account of the tremendous progresses that have been achieved in this field and to highlight the increasing importance of primary amine-based thioureas as organocatalysts in synthetic chemistry.
